name: Mitophagy enhancers surveillance pass for June 10, 2026

scope.question: What changed for Mitophagy Enhancers since the 2026-05-24 public baseline, and does any change justify a public update?

summary: Ran a mitophagy-specific surveillance pass from the 2026-05-24 baseline through 2026-06-10. The scoped trial audit found four registry-linked interventional studies: three result-bearing urolithin A studies represented by PubMed sources and one completed frail older-adult registry with no posted results. Current ClinicalTrials.gov checks found no newly posted results for the frail older-adult trial and no registry-result posting for ENERGIZE, ATLAS, or MitoImmune beyond their published papers. PubMed same-window searches found no stage-changing older-human mitophagy result, USP30 clinical result, or correction. The pass did surface missed source-completeness items: a human urolithin A renal-calculi safety report, a young-athlete urolithin A RCT linked to NCT04783207, and several active or completed-no-results urolithin A registries. Because these are safety, registry, and taxonomy completeness issues rather than a fresh public evidence delta, no public source, finding, outlook, activity item, or candidate bundle was staged. A first coverage assessment was added and recommends coverage repair before ordinary surveillance resumes.

materiality_decision.rationale: The current evidence stage remains low-confidence human_biomarker_signal: the pass found no new result that changes stage, confidence, momentum, or outlook language. However, the mitophagy-specific public outlook is still a compact pre-support-map record, active urolithin A registries are incomplete for this track, current registry dates differ or are unstaged for some published trials, and a pre-baseline human renal-calculi safety source has not been reconciled. These source-completeness issues should be handled in coverage_repair rather than forced into an ordinary surveillance update.

search_log.summary: Found four registry-linked interventional studies, all with trial_details. One completed or past-completion registry has no posted results: NCT06556706. Three completed studies with posted PubMed results lacked local registry check dates: NCT03283462, NCT03464500, and NCT05735886.

ENERGIZE, ATLAS, MitoImmune, and the frail older-adult Mitopure trial all remain completed. NCT06556706 still has no posted registry results after its 2025-04-25 actual completion. The three result-bearing published trials also have no ClinicalTrials.gov results section, so the public evidence layer remains anchored to PubMed sources.

Found relevant no-results or watchlist urolithin A records including NCT06274749, NCT07231783, NCT05814705, NCT04783207, NCT05921266, NCT06990256, NCT07060898, NCT07377136, and NCT04985630. These are active, completed-no-results, methods, cognition, metabolic, obesity, sleep, or young-athlete contexts and should be reconciled in coverage repair.

search_log.query: USP30 OR ST-539 OR MF-094 OR MF094 OR mitophagy
search_log.summary: Found no USP30 intervention trial and no aging-relevant posted mitophagy-enhancer result. Close records were observational mitophagy studies, ICU or cardiovascular disease contexts, or unrelated mitochondrial-function records.

search_log.query: (urolithin A OR urolithin-a OR Mitopure OR USP30 OR ST-539 OR MF-094 OR MF094) AND aging, older, frailty, sarcopenia, muscle, immune, longevity, lifespan, healthspan, trial, human, or clinical terms; publication date 2026-05-24 to 2026-06-10
search_log.summary: Returned seven urolithin/USP30-adjacent records. Close records included PMID 42058113, PMID 42261160, PMID 42240896, and PMID 41722622. None changed the public evidence stage, but PMID 42058113 is a safety-source completeness issue and PMID 42261160 is review-level mitophagy/urolithin context.

search_log.query: (mitophagy OR mitochondrial quality control) AND aging, older, frailty, sarcopenia, muscle, immune, longevity, lifespan, or healthspan; publication date 2026-05-24 to 2026-06-10
search_log.summary: Returned 30 broad mitophagy or mitochondrial-quality-control records. Close titles were review, preclinical disease-model, toxicology, cancer, wound-healing, cardiac-aging, cachexia, or mechanism papers rather than human mitophagy-enhancer aging results.

Found PMID 40839339 linked to NCT04783207. It reports a 42-participant highly trained young male runner urolithin A trial with recovery and biomarker signals but no significant 3000 m performance improvement; it is context for coverage repair, not an aging-stage-changing mitophagy result.

Confirmed the renal-calculi report describes chemically confirmed urolithin A in urinary stones from eight patients aged 59 to 87, mostly with dietary ellagitannin exposure and no reported urolithin A supplement use among the six patients with exposure histories. This is safety/durability context requiring coverage repair, not immediate efficacy-stage movement.

trial_watch_checks.summary: ENERGIZE remains completed with no ClinicalTrials.gov results section; the result-bearing older-adult PubMed source remains the public evidence anchor, but registry dates and check metadata should be reconciled during coverage repair.

ATLAS remains completed with no ClinicalTrials.gov results section; the result-bearing middle-aged-adult PubMed source remains represented, but registry metadata is incomplete in the local study record.
MitoImmune remains completed with no ClinicalTrials.gov results section; the result-bearing PubMed source remains represented, but registry metadata is incomplete in the local study record.
The frail older-adult Mitopure trial remains completed with 26 actual participants and no posted registry results; this stays the key mitophagy result-watch item.

excluded_sources.title: First Reported Human Cases of Urolithin A Renal Calculi
excluded_sources.reason: Human safety signal for urolithin A exposure and urinary-stone chemistry, but it was e-published and PubMed-indexed before the May 24 baseline and mostly involved dietary exposure rather than supplement use; handle through safety/durability coverage repair rather than a same-window public update.

excluded_sources.title: Evaluating the Impact of Urolithin A Supplementation on Running Performance, Recovery, and Mitochondrial Biomarkers in Highly Trained Male Distance Runners
excluded_sources.reason: Result-bearing urolithin A RCT linked to NCT04783207, but the population is young highly trained male athletes and the study did not significantly improve the 3000 m performance endpoint; coverage repair should decide whether to stage or explicitly exclude it.

excluded_sources.title: Rejuvenating Inter-organellar Communication Via Mitophagy in Ageing and Neurodegeneration
excluded_sources.reason: Review-level mitophagy and urolithin A mechanism context. It does not add primary human aging evidence or change the public stage.

excluded_sources.title: Autophagy activation by urolithin-a derivative UA-36 mitigates Friedreich's ataxia pathologies induced by frataxin deficiency
excluded_sources.reason: Preclinical Friedreich's ataxia model using a urolithin A derivative, not direct urolithin A supplementation, older-human aging evidence, or an aging mitophagy-enhancer result.

excluded_sources.title: Age-related sarcopenia and the gut microbiome: mechanistic insights into the gut-muscle axis and potential microbiome based therapeutic interventions
excluded_sources.reason: Review-level gut-muscle-axis and urolithin A context; useful for taxonomy with microbiome-metabolite restoration but not a new mitophagy result.

excluded_sources.title: Effects of Urolithin A Supplementation on Glucose Metabolism in Healthy Adults 55 >= Years Old
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting 180-participant older-adult urolithin A trial with glucose, mitochondrial respiration, brain oxidative metabolism, and microbiome endpoints; no posted results.

excluded_sources.title: The Effect of Urolithin A (Mitopure) Supplementation on Muscle Strength in Healthy Middle-Aged Adults
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting 120-participant middle-aged-adult muscle-strength trial with no posted results; relevant to future mitophagy coverage but not a current public update.

excluded_sources.title: The Effects of Protein Supplementation With or Without Urolithin A During Single-leg Immobilization
excluded_sources.reason: Active-not-recruiting immobilization and mitochondrial-respiration study with no posted results; coverage repair should decide whether it belongs under mitophagy, muscle disuse, or nutrition context.

excluded_sources.title: Effects of Mitopure (Urolithin A) on Skeletal Muscle Function, Iron Metabolism and Endurance Performance
excluded_sources.reason: Completed registry has a linked PubMed result in young trained male runners. It is result-bearing but not older-adult or healthy-aging evidence; coverage repair should explicitly stage or exclude it.

excluded_sources.title: Urolithin A Supplementation in Middle-aged Adults With Obesity
excluded_sources.reason: Completed 50-participant middle-aged-obesity vascular and mitochondrial-biomarker study with no posted results.

excluded_sources.title: Evaluation of Urolithin A and Fisetin on Improving Sleep and Aging Biomarkers
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting urolithin A/fisetin sleep and aging-biomarker trial with no posted results and combination-intervention attribution limits.

excluded_sources.title: Impact of a Novel Brain Longevity Supplement Containing the Postbiotic Urolithin A (Mitopure) on Cognitive Function and Related Health Outcomes
excluded_sources.reason: Active-not-recruiting cognition and brain-longevity supplement trial with no posted results and a multi-ingredient product boundary.

excluded_sources.title: Influence of the Urolithin A on the Population With BMI Higher Than 30 During Restricted Eating
excluded_sources.reason: Enrolling-by-invitation BMI and restricted-eating urolithin A trial with muscle, metabolic, inflammatory, vascular, and epigenetic-aging endpoints but no posted results.

excluded_sources.title: The Mitopure Challenge to Detect Levels of Urolithin A in Dried Blood Spots
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting methods and exposure-detection registry for dried blood spot urolithin A measurement; useful methods context, not an efficacy or safety result.

notes: This is the first mitophagy-specific surveillance record; prior urolithin A registry checks were partly covered in microbiome-metabolite and selective-autophagy surveillance.
PMID 42058113 is safety-relevant but was e-published and PubMed-indexed before the May 24 mitophagy baseline; it is a missed-source coverage gap rather than a fresh same-window delta.
The public mitophagy outlook still lacks the richer evidence-map and support-map structure used by newer outlooks.
next_actions: Run coverage_repair for mitophagy enhancers before ordinary surveillance resumes.
During coverage repair, reconcile active urolithin A registries, the young-athlete NCT04783207 result, renal-calculi safety context, and registry metadata for ENERGIZE, ATLAS, and MitoImmune.
Create a public bundle only if coverage repair changes public source, study, finding, support-map, safety, taxonomy, or outlook wording.
